skill.name: amazon-listing-competitor-analysis-skill
skill.description: Analyze Amazon competitor listings by ASIN to produce structured competitive intelligence and strategic opportunity points for your go-to-market strategy.
skill.summary: Extract and analyze Amazon competitor listing data via BrowserAct API to uncover content gaps, keyword opportunities, and market positioning insights.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 85 patterns across 2 files, producing a risk score of 100/100. After evaluation, the vast majority are false positives: network calls target only the BrowserAct API (the skill's own service), environment variable access reads a single documented API key sent exclusively to its originating service, and SKILL.md backtick detections are all Markdown formatting, not executable shell commands. The CRITICAL heuristic (code execution + network + credential access) describes a legitimate API client pattern. One minor confirmed finding: the skill executes a Python script via shell command with user-supplied ASIN argument, but input is an alphanumeric ASIN identifier with negligible injection surface. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The Python script reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable and sends it as a Bearer token in Authorization headers to api.browseract.com. The key is used exclusively to authenticate with its own issuing service. No exfiltration to third parties. Standard API client pattern.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Execution with User Input
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI agent to execute a Python script via shell command with a user-supplied ASIN argument. The ASIN parameter is passed as a command-line argument. ASINs are alphanumeric Amazon identifiers (e.g., B0CS62LY6P) with a constrained character set, making command injection impractical. Nonetheless, any shell execution with user input warrants documentation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs in Source
low_findings.description: Both the Python script and SKILL.md contain hardcoded URLs pointing to browseract.com (API endpoint, integrations page, plan upgrade page) and amazon.com (default marketplace). All URLs point to the skill's own service or the documented target platform. No unknown or suspicious domains.

dangerous_patterns.title: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged the combination of Python script execution, HTTP requests to external APIs, and environment variable credential access as a CRITICAL heuristic match. After evaluation, this is a benign pattern: the skill is an API client that must run code, make network calls to its own service, and authenticate with its own API key. This is the expected architecture for any API-integrated skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 40 locations in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. Every flagged instance is Markdown formatting: inline code spans using single backticks and fenced code blocks using triple backticks. None of these are executable shell constructs. This is a systematic false positive in the static analyzer's pattern matching for documentation files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 locations in SKILL.md as weak cryptographic algorithms. Review of the flagged lines shows competitive analysis framework headings and field names (e.g., 'keyword placement', 'title formula', 'ASIN'). No cryptography is implemented anywhere in this skill. These are false positives from pattern matching on hash-like or encoding-like substrings in natural language text.

content.user_title: Analyze Amazon Competitor Listings and Find Market Gaps
content.value_statement: Amazon sellers struggle to understand why competitor listings outperform theirs and where market opportunities exist. This skill extracts real competitor listing data via BrowserAct API and applies a structured analysis framework to uncover content gaps, keyword opportunities, and strategic positioning insights you can act on.
actual_capabilities: Extract full Amazon listing data (title, bullets, description, images, reviews, ratings) for any ASIN via BrowserAct API
Analyze competitor keyword placement across title, bullets, and description sections
Mine customer reviews to identify buyer-validated selling points and unmet needs
Evaluate visual content strategy including image sequence narrative and infographic usage
Generate structured competitive intelligence reports with actionable opportunity points
Support cross-marketplace analysis by running the same ASIN against different regional Amazon URLs
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key with available task quota
Data quality depends on the BrowserAct extraction template output
Only analyzes one competitor ASIN at a time
Cannot access Amazon Advertising data or internal sales metrics

use_cases.title: Pre-Launch Competitive Research
use_cases.description: Before launching a new product, analyze the top competitor listing to understand what content, keywords, and visual strategies are working, then identify gaps your product can fill.

use_cases.title: Listing Optimization Audit
use_cases.description: Compare your existing listing against a higher-ranked competitor to find specific improvements in title structure, bullet point prioritization, keyword placement, and visual content strategy.

use_cases.title: Market Opportunity Discovery
use_cases.description: Mine competitor reviews for recurring customer complaints and unmet needs, then translate those gaps into new product features, bundles, or positioning angles for your brand.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Competitor Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the Amazon listing for ASIN B0CS62LY6P and give me a summary of what this competitor does well and where there are gaps I can exploit.
prompt_templates.scenario: You found a competitor product and want a fast strategic read before deeper analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword Strategy Deep-Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the listing data for ASIN B0CS62LY6P and do a full keyword placement audit. Map which keywords appear in the title, bullets, and description. Then identify keyword gaps based on the review language and category context.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are planning your Amazon SEO strategy and need to understand how a top competitor structures their keywords.

prompt_templates.title: Review Mining for Product Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Pull the reviews for ASIN B0CS62LY6P and mine them for: (1) what buyers consistently praise that the listing underplays, (2) recurring complaints or wish-list items from 3-star reviews, and (3) unmet needs I could address with a new product variant or bundle.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to find product improvement or new SKU ideas from real customer feedback on competitor products.

prompt_templates.title: Full Competitive Battle Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete competitive analysis on ASIN B0CS62LY6P using the full framework: content strategy, keyword placement, visual strategy, buyer-validated selling points, unmet needs, content/keyword/visual gaps, and strategic opportunity points for my brand. My product is a [describe your product category] and I compete on [price/quality/feature/design].
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a comprehensive competitive intelligence report to inform your go-to-market strategy.

output_examples.input: Analyze ASIN B0CS62LY6P for competitive gaps
output_examples.output:
Competitor ASIN: B0CS62LY6P | Brand: TechPro | BSR: #342 in Kitchen | Rating: 4.3 (1,247 reviews)

✅ WHAT THIS COMPETITOR DOES RIGHT
- Title formula: Brand + Key Feature + Product Type + Size/Variant
- Bullet priority: Leads with material quality, follows with ease of use, then versatility
- Keyword Placement: Primary terms in title and first two bullets, long-tail terms spread across remaining bullets

🕳️ MARKET GAPS
- Content gap: No mention of dishwasher safety despite reviews asking about it
- Keyword gap: "BPA-free" and "eco-friendly" appear in reviews but not in listing copy
- Visual gap: No size comparison or in-use lifestyle images in the gallery

🎯 YOUR OPPORTUNITIES
- Own the "safe materials" angle with BPA-free and dishwasher-safe messaging
- Add comparison chart imagery showing size against common objects
- Create a bundle variant addressing the "wish it came with a case" complaint from reviews

output_examples.input: What keywords is this competitor ranking for?
output_examples.output:
Keyword Placement Map for ASIN B0CS62LY6P:

Title (first 80 chars): "stainless steel," "insulated," "water bottle"
Bullets 1-2: "double-wall vacuum," "keeps cold 24hrs," "leak-proof"
Bullets 3-5: "BPA-free," "easy clean," "fits cup holder," "gift-ready"
Description/A+: "powder-coated," "sweat-free," "outdoor," "commuter"

Uncovered terms from reviews: "dishwasher safe," "replacement lid," "lightweight"

These uncovered terms represent your keyword gap opportunities.

best_practices: Always verify that the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before invoking the script, and ask the user for it if missing
Use the full competitive analysis framework in Phase 2 to ensure consistent, data-grounded insights rather than generic observations
Match the marketplace URL to the correct Amazon regional site for the ASIN being analyzed to ensure accurate data extraction
anti_patterns: Do not treat the analyzed ASIN as a listing to edit or rewrite — it is evidence for competitive research, not your own product page
Do not make claims about the competitor that are not grounded in extracted data fields or quoted review text
Do not retry the script more than once on failure without checking for authorization or concurrency limit errors first

faq.question: What is an ASIN and where do I find it?
faq.answer: An ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) is a unique product identifier on Amazon. You can find it in the product URL or in the Product Information section of any Amazon listing page.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key. You can get one by signing up at browseract.com and navigating to the Integrations page. The skill will prompt you for the key if it is not configured.

faq.question: How long does the analysis take to complete?
faq.answer: The extraction phase typically takes several minutes as it involves automated browser operations. The script outputs status logs with timestamps so you can monitor progress. The AI analysis phase runs immediately after data is received.

faq.question: Can I analyze listings from Amazon marketplaces outside the US?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify any Amazon marketplace URL such as amazon.de, amazon.co.uk, or amazon.co.jp. This enables cross-marketplace competitive research.

faq.question: What if the script fails or times out?
faq.answer: The script has built-in error handling for authorization issues and concurrency limits. For other failures, one automatic retry is allowed. If the second attempt also fails, the error message will be reported to you directly.

faq.question: Is my BrowserAct API key shared with anyone else?
faq.answer: No, your API key is only sent to the BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com) to authenticate your requests. It is never transmitted to any other service or stored by this skill.
